text.title: SoW Traced-Assumptions Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that make every SoW clause defendable against post-signature scope disputes.

rule.statement: Every in-scope deliverable line in the SoW body MUST carry an inline assumption ID in the format `[A-NN]` (e.g., `[A-07]`), placed at the end of the clause. A deliverable line without an ID is a draft, not a SoW item.
rule.rationale: Without per-line IDs, a dispute cannot be argued at the clause level — only at the whole-SoW level, which is what kills margin. ID-per-clause forces the BA to map each commitment to its trace before sign-off.

rule.statement: The SoW MUST ship with an `assumptions.md` appendix where each `[A-NN]` maps to: (a) a concrete artefact path or URL, (b) the artefact section/timestamp, (c) one-line restatement of the assumption, (d) the named stakeholder who confirmed it. Any `[A-NN]` referenced in the body but missing from the appendix blocks sign-off.
rule.rationale: The trace IS the defence. A bare assumption number with no source is the same as no source; the appendix is the artefact the BA shows when a client says "we never agreed to that".

rule.statement: Every exclusion clause ("the following is OUT of scope: ...") MUST also carry an `[A-NN]` ID. Exclusions are commitments in reverse and disputed just as often as inclusions.
rule.rationale: Outsource shops over-document inclusions and under-document exclusions; the costly disputes are usually "but we thought X was included" — i.e., a missing exclusion trace. IDs on exclusions force symmetric rigour.

rule.statement: Every pricing-model assumption (volumes, peak load, third-party SLAs, integration counts) MUST be a separate `[A-NN]` line in the appendix, even when it does not appear in the SoW body. The fixed price is conditioned on these assumptions; a breach is a change-request trigger.
rule.rationale: Pricing assumptions are where fixed-price engagements bleed. Tying the price to enumerated IDs lets the BA invoke the change-request protocol the moment one is violated, instead of arguing intent.

rule.statement: The SoW MUST include a "Change Request Protocol" clause that explicitly requires any future scope change to cite the affected `[A-NN]` ID(s) and the artefact line that supersedes them. A change request without an ID citation is rejected by the protocol.
rule.rationale: Without a citation rule, the trace is decorative. Tying the change protocol to assumption IDs makes the traceability operational rather than archival.

rule.statement: If the Applies-If preconditions fail OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or to no methodology at all).
rule.rationale: Mis-application of a methodology produces worse artefacts than no methodology at all because downstream consumers trust the schema and act on bad data. The skip-record makes the non-application traceable so future audits can detect drift.

example.code:
## In-scope deliverables
- Build customer onboarding flow with email + phone verification, up to 3 form steps. [A-04] - Integrate single payment provider (Stripe) using the Payment Intents API. [A-07]
## Out of scope
- Multi-currency support; system is single-currency (EUR) for v1. [A-12]
## Appendix: assumptions.md
| ID | Source artefact | Section / timestamp | Statement | Confirmed by | |----|-----------------|---------------------|-----------|--------------| | A-04 | interviews/2026-04-15-vp-ops.md | 00:12:30 transcript | Onboarding will not exceed 3 form steps for v1 | Maria Lopes (VP Ops) | | A-07 | decisions/payments-provider-2026-04-22.md | "Decision" paragraph | Stripe only for v1; Adyen deferred | Tomás Silva (CFO) | | A-12 | reqs/non-functional.md | line 47 | v1 supports EUR only | Maria Lopes (VP Ops) |
example.note: Each clause is defendable. A dispute on "we expected multi-currency" lands on A-12 with a named source, not on a verbal argument.

example.code:
## In-scope deliverables
- Build a customer onboarding flow. - Integrate payments.
## Assumptions
- Standard scope assumptions apply.
example.note: Unbounded inclusions, no IDs, no appendix entries. Every clause is a future dispute waiting to happen — the SoW protects nothing.
